tossups.question: A two-story residence of this name was decorated with memorabilia of the 1980 Olympics and was built to honor the missing brother of architect Yusif Jafarov in Ganja, Azerbaijan. Ruth and Bill Lucas designed a building of this name in the suburb of Castlecrag that drew on the ideas of the Sydney School. A castle-like dwelling of this name was built by David H. Brown on the shores of Kootenay Lake in British Columbia. A Hudson Square condominium complex named for the word (*) “urban” and this phrase was one starchitect’s last project before his 2005 death, though residence in it declined after the New York Department of Sanitation building was built across the street. The landscape surrounding a building of this name was modeled on Poussin’s The Funeral of Phocion. A building with this name lies near a cylindrical sculpture by Donald Judd and a similar rectangular “brick” building. For 10 points, give this common name of structures including a New Canaan, Philip Johnson-designed domicile whose owners proverbially shouldn’t throw stones.
tossups.question_sanitized: A two-story residence of this name was decorated with memorabilia of the 1980 Olympics and was built to honor the missing brother of architect Yusif Jafarov in Ganja, Azerbaijan. Ruth and Bill Lucas designed a building of this name in the suburb of Castlecrag that drew on the ideas of the Sydney School. A castle-like dwelling of this name was built by David H. Brown on the shores of Kootenay Lake in British Columbia. A Hudson Square condominium complex named for the word (*) "urban" and this phrase was one starchitect's last project before his 2005 death, though residence in it declined after the New York Department of Sanitation building was built across the street. The landscape surrounding a building of this name was modeled on Poussin's The Funeral of Phocion. A building with this name lies near a cylindrical sculpture by Donald Judd and a similar rectangular "brick" building. For 10 points, give this common name of structures including a New Canaan, Philip Johnson-designed domicile whose owners proverbially shouldn't throw stones.

tossups.question: Several angles of this building were compared with similar shots of an older building in Robin Hill’s June 2015 photography series Side by Side. Fujiko Nakaya’s project Veil used six hidden nozzles to envelop this building with mist for 15 minutes out of every hour. One of the three versions of Nicolas Poussin’s The Funeral of Phocion rests on an easel mount in this building, where it serves as a sort of screen that sets off a white carpeted living room decorated with a Mies van der Rohe leather furniture set. An underground art gallery styled after Agamemnon’s Tomb lies near this building and contains the collection its architect assembled with (*) David Whitney. The floor of this building is raised brick in a herringbone pattern, except for its small bathroom enclosed in a brick cylinder. For 10 points, name this house which was designed by Philip Johnson to replace walls with windows.
tossups.question_sanitized: Several angles of this building were compared with similar shots of an older building in Robin Hill's June 2015 photography series Side by Side. Fujiko Nakaya's project Veil used six hidden nozzles to envelop this building with mist for 15 minutes out of every hour. One of the three versions of Nicolas Poussin's The Funeral of Phocion rests on an easel mount in this building, where it serves as a sort of screen that sets off a white carpeted living room decorated with a Mies van der Rohe leather furniture set. An underground art gallery styled after Agamemnon's Tomb lies near this building and contains the collection its architect assembled with (*) David Whitney. The floor of this building is raised brick in a herringbone pattern, except for its small bathroom enclosed in a brick cylinder. For 10 points, name this house which was designed by Philip Johnson to replace walls with windows.

bonuses.leadin: This teacher praised the Bauhaus as the “House of the White Man” as part of his devotion to the racist ideology of Mazdaznan. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This teacher praised the Bauhaus as the "House of the White Man" as part of his devotion to the racist ideology of Mazdaznan. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this teacher of the Preliminary Course at the Bauhaus for its first three years. This artist left the Bauhaus as the school tended away from his expressionist tendencies and towards industrial crafts.

Itten also developed a Bauhaus workshop for painting this material. This material names both a pavilion and a chain letter created by Bruno Taut, and was used less expressionistically alongside steel in Mies van der Rohe’s towers.

Within the Deutscher Werkbund, Taut’s Expressionist faction was opposed to an industrialist one led by Peter Behrens, who designed a turbine factory for this company featuring 50-foot glass windows on its ends.
parts_sanitized: Name this teacher of the Preliminary Course at the Bauhaus for its first three years. This artist left the Bauhaus as the school tended away from his expressionist tendencies and towards industrial crafts.

Itten also developed a Bauhaus workshop for painting this material. This material names both a pavilion and a chain letter created by Bruno Taut, and was used less expressionistically alongside steel in Mies van der Rohe's towers.

Within the Deutscher Werkbund, Taut's Expressionist faction was opposed to an industrialist one led by Peter Behrens, who designed a turbine factory for this company featuring 50-foot glass windows on its ends.
answers_sanitized: glass [or Glas; accept Glass Pavilion or Glass House]

bonuses.leadin: Local designer Jens Risom restored the interior of a 1960s pool house in this state, the Gores Pavilion. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Local designer Jens Risom restored the interior of a 1960s pool house in this state, the Gores Pavilion. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this non-Massachusetts state where the “Harvard Five” were active. An angular, red-and-black building called “Da Monsta” stands outside another house in this state that was modeled on the Farnsworth House.

Philip Johnson was a member of the “Harvard Five” along with this architect, whose International Style house in New Canaan contained Alexander Calder’s sculpture Black Beast II. This architect also designed IBM’s Selectric Typewriter.

Marcel Breuer, another member of the “Harvard Five,” studied at this institution. The Farnsworth House was designed by Ludwig Mies van der Rohe (“meese van der roh”), who led this institution before it was closed by the Nazis.
parts_sanitized: Name this non-Massachusetts state where the "Harvard Five" were active. An angular, red-and-black building called "Da Monsta" stands outside another house in this state that was modeled on the Farnsworth House.

Philip Johnson was a member of the "Harvard Five" along with this architect, whose International Style house in New Canaan contained Alexander Calder's sculpture Black Beast II. This architect also designed IBM's Selectric Typewriter.

Marcel Breuer, another member of the "Harvard Five," studied at this institution. The Farnsworth House was designed by Ludwig Mies van der Rohe ("meese van der roh"), who led this institution before it was closed by the Nazis.
answers: Connecticut [or CT] (The last line refers to Philip Johnson’s Glass House.)
answers_sanitized: Connecticut [or CT] (The last line refers to Philip Johnson's Glass House.)

bonuses.leadin: This award's 2022 winner was inspired by the baobab (BAU-bab) tree to create an installation of twelve colorful towers for the Coachella Festival in 2019. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this prestigious international architecture prize established by an entrepreneur who also names a Frank Gehry-designed Pavilion in Chicago's Millennium Park.

This designer of the Xylem Pavilion in Montana won the 2022 Pritzker Prize. His first building, a primary school that offers both ventilation and protection from the sun, is in his home village of Gando, Burkina Faso.

The first-ever Pritzker Prize was awarded to this architect. He designed PPG Place in Pittsburgh with John Burgee and built a residence in New Canaan (KAY-nun), Connecticut named for a certain material.
answers: Philip (Cortelyou) Johnson [The personal residence is the Glass House]
answers_sanitized: Pritzker Architecture Prize [accept the Pritzker Pavillion]
Philip (Cortelyou) Johnson [The personal residence is the Glass House]

The first-ever Pritzker Prize was awarded to this architect. He designed PPG Place in Pittsburgh with John Burgee and built a residence in New Canaan (KAY-nun), Connecticut named for a certain material.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer the following about architects' predilections for various materials and colors.
parts: Philip Johnson liked this material so much he designed for himself a steel-framed personal residence named for it. He was influenced by Ludwig Mies van der Rohe's use of it in the Farnsworth House.
Brutalist architecture makes heavy use of this material, deriving its name from a term used by Le Corbusier referring to its "raw" manifestation.
This architect loves using the color white for the exteriors of his buildings, including Barcelona's Museum of Contemporary Art and New Harmony's Atheneum.

bonuses.leadin: This building somehow scored an abysmal 3 out of 100 for its Energy Star rating, and the asshole architect of this building insisted that the blinds only have three possible settings. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this corporate modernist skyscraper whose original plans had to be scrapped because building codes require structural material to be fireproofed.
This architect designed the Four Seasons restaurant for the Seagram Building, and also designed the Crystal Cathedral and Pittsburgh's PPG Place.
Johnson died in this building he designed to be his primary residence. The "Da Monsta" visitor's pavilion was built on the Connecticut grounds of this building.

bonuses.leadin: This architect employed a zig-zag stepback design, consisting of what he termed "zogs," for the IDS Center in Minneapolis. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this American architect who collaborated with Mies van der Rohe on the Seagram Building.
Johnson borrowed from Mies's Farnsworth House for the design of this Connecticut building, his personal residence. Its exterior is almost entirely composed of a transparent medium.
Robert Schuller founded a ministry named for this Johnson structure, a religious building in Garden Grove, California, that houses one of the largest pipe organs in the world.
